page: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance is also strong, placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, achieve top-tier throughput. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. For lightweight language model inference, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 440.91 tokens per second on a small 135M model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F32-16ams_v7 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of local storage. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external premium storage attachment. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 16.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation speeds. Its memory performance is highly rated, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. The F32-16ams_v7 is designed for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC processor at 4.5 GHz, 256 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for databases and caching.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high-frequency dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a substantial memory-to-CPU ratio for demanding enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage included, requiring external premium storage solutions
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
